info.title: API V1
info.description:
A RestFull API for Decidim, to be able to CRUD resources from Decidim.  
_current version: 0.2.9_

## Authentication
[Get a token](https://octree-gva.github.io/decidim-rest-full/category/authentication) from our `/oauth/token` routes,
following OAuth specs on Credential Flows or Resource Owner Password Credentials Flow.

### Permissions
A permission system is attached to the created OAuth application, that is designed in two levels:

- **scope**: a broad permission to access a collection of endpoints
- **abilities**: a fine grained permission system that allow actions.

The scopes and abilities are manageable in your System Admin Panel.

### Multi-tenant
Decidim is multi-tenant, and this API supports it.
- The **`system` scope** endpoints are available in any tenant
- The tenant `host` attribute will be used to guess which tenant you are requesting.
  For example, given a tenant `example.org` and `foobar.org`, the endpoint
  * `example.org/oauth/token` will ask a token for the example.org organization
  * `foobar.org/oauth/token` for foobar.org.

tags.name: API
tags.description: Entry point and metadata. No authentication required.

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description:
Use OAuth to get tokens and interact with the API. You can use machine-to-machine tokens, or user token directly with the API.
* **Machine-to-machine**: Client Credential Flow
* **User**: Resource Owner Password Credential Flow, with **impersonation** or **login**

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description:
Organization controls the main configuration of the platform. 
As you can host many Decidim in the same platform, organizations allows you to setup: 

- host: the domain name used by your decidim
- secondary_hosts: other domain names, used for redirecting to the correct organization

The current organization is guessed from the host of the request, you can thus query the API
on different hosts to gather organization's related data.

tags.name: Spaces
tags.description: Search and list participatory spaces (e.g. Assemblies, Participatory Processes).

tags.name: Components
tags.description: Search and list components (proposals, meetings, blogs, etc.) in spaces.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Get information about participants, and manage own data.

tags.name: Blogs
tags.description: List and get blog posts (articles) from blog components.

tags.name: Proposals
tags.description: List and get proposals; create, update, publish, and delete draft proposals; vote on proposals.

credentialFlowBearer.description:
Authorization via service-to-service credentials flow.
Use this for machine clients.
[Learn more here](https://octree-gva.github.io/decidim-rest-full/user_documentation/auth/client-credential-flow)

resourceOwnerFlowBearer.description:
Authorization via resource owner flow.
Use this for user-based authentication
[Learn more here](https://octree-gva.github.io/decidim-rest-full/user_documentation/auth/user-credentials-flow)

username.description: User nickname, unique and at least 6 alphanumeric chars.

id.description: User id, will find over id and ignore username. Fails if register_on_missing=true.

register_on_missing.description: Register the user if it does not exists. Default: false

accept_tos_on_register.description: Accept the TOS on registration, used only if register_on_missing=true. Default: false

skip_confirmation_on_register.description: Skip email confirmation on creation, used only if register_on_missing=true. Default: false

email.description: User email to use on registration. used only if register_on_missing=true. Default to <username>@example.org

name.description: User name. Used only if register_on_missing=true. Default to username

proposal_component.title: Proposal Component

participatory_space_id.description: Associate space id. Part of the polymorphic association (participatory_space_type,participatory_space_id)

can_create_proposals.description: If the current user can create proposal (component allows, and user did not reach publication limit)

can_vote.description: If the current user can vote on the component

can_comment.description: If the current user comment on the component

geocoding_enabled.description: If the component needs a map to display its resources

attachments_allowed.description: If the component allows to attach files to resources

collaborative_drafts_enabled.description: If you can create collaborative draft for the proposal

comments_enabled.description: If you can comment on proposals

participatory_texts_enabled.description: If proposals are based on a text modification

proposal_edit_before_minutes.description: Time in minute participant can edit the proposal

proposal_edit_time.description: Type of restriction for proposal edition

proposal_limit.description: Max proposal per participant. No maximum if value is 0

resources_permissions_enabled.description: If authorizations can be defined per proposal

vote_limit.description: Max Number of vote per participant. 0 if no limit

votes_enabled.description: If votes on proposal are enabled

creation_enabled.description: If participant can create proposal are enabled

amendment_creation_enabled.description: If participant can propose an amendment to a proposal

amendment_reaction_enabled.description: If participant can react to an amendment of a proposal

amendment_promotion_enabled.description: If participant choose an amendment to replace their initial proposal

votes.title: Proposal Vote Weights Options
votes.description: Vote weight, if can_vote is true.

weight.description: Value to add to the vote. 0 for abstention

proposal_component.description:
A proposal component can host proposals from participants, and official proposals (proposals from the organization).
This component have many metadatas that explain what are the restrictions regarding proposing, voting, commenting, amending or endorsing. 

Features toggles: 
- `can_create_proposals`: If participants can create proposals
- `can_vote`: If participants can vote
- `can_comment`: If participants can comments
- .... and some more

send_welcome_notification.description: True if welcome email is sent to users

users_registration_mode.description:
Define user registration mode: 
- `enabled`: Enable users registration
- `existing`: Existing users will be able to login. Registration will be disabled.
- `disabled`: No registration enabled

force_users_to_authenticate_before_acces.description: Force users to authenticate before accessing the organization (disabled if users_registration_mode is `disabled`)

enable_participatory_space_filters.description: Display areas and scopes filter in public views.

enable_machine_translations.description: Enable machine translations (must be configured, see [Using machine translations](https://docs.decidim.org/en/develop/develop/machine_translations.html))

user_groups_enabled.description: Enable user groups in public views

rich_text_editor_in_public_views.description: Enable rich text editor in public views

unconfirmed_host.description: If host update is pending, unconfirmed host for the organization

user_extended_data.title: User extended data
user_extended_data.description:
Hash of values attached to a user. These values won't be
displayed to admins or users, consider this as an internal
data payload.

blocked.description: If the user is blocked, and need to be unblocked to signin

locked.description: If the user is locked, and need to click on the mail link to unlock

name.description: User name, use to display the Profile identity. Public

nickname.description: User nickname, unique identifier for the user. Public

personal_url.description: Personal website URL or social link. Public

about.description: Short bio of the user. Public

locale.description: User locale. Fallback to default locale of the organization. Private

get.description: Detail a draft proposal. Raise HTTP 404 error if no draft is created for now.

delete.description: Withdraw (delete) a draft proposal. This action cannot be undone.

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to  update a draft proposal associated with your application ID.
Drafts updated via this API are not visible in the Decidim front-end, and drafts created from the Decidim application are not editable through the API.
Therefore, any draft you create here is new and tied to your application's credentials.

### Example Request

```http
PUT /public/assemblies/12/2319/proposals/draft
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_bearer_token

{
  "title": "My valid title"
}
```
## Access Requirements

* Authentication: This endpoint requires an impersonation token. You must create drafts on behalf of a participant; drafts cannot be created using a service token (credential_token).

## Error Handling

* Field Errors: Only errors related to the fields you're updating will be returned.
* Publishable Status: To determine if the draft is publishable, check the data.meta.publishable field in the response.

### Example response
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "12345",
    "type": "proposal",
    "attributes": {
      "title": "My valid title",
      "body": null
    },
    "meta": {
      "publishable": false
    }
  }
}
```
In this example, the title is valid, so the server returns a 200 OK status.
However, since the body is blank, meta.publishable is false, indicating that the draft is not yet ready for publication.

title.fr: This is a valid proposal title sample

data.title: Update Draft Proposal Payload Data

locale.description: Locale of the draft. default to user locale

get.description: Show organization

put.description:
This endpoint allows you to update an organization.

### Update host
To update the host, send in your payload the `host` attribute. It will be saved as an `unconfirmed_host` extended data attribute. 
Once saved, a job will be enqueued to reverse DNS the unconfirmed host before actually updating the host.
The `host` attribute must be unique across all organizations.
More information on this update process is documented in the [Safe host update](https://octree-gva.github.io/decidim-rest-full/dev/update-hosts) page.

### Update name
To update the name, send in your payload the `name` attribute.
The `name` attribute must be unique across all organizations.

value.error_description: Unconfirmed host has already been taken
Name en has already been taken
